title: centaurea ovina subsp. hohenackeri
intro: Centaurea ovina subsp. hohenackeri, also known as the Alpine knapweed, is a subspecies of the Centaurea ovina plant, a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Centaurea ovina subsp. hohenackeri can be prepared for herbal use in the form of teas, infusions, or dried flower extracts, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
